About Robert Cromwell, "Baron Cromwell of Carleton"
Robert Cromwell was born circa 1390 at of Carleton-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England. He died on 29 March 1461 at Battle of Towton, Towton, Yorkshi …

nameMeanings.name: Cromwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire, from Old English crumb ‘bent, crooked’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Cromwell Lord of Carleton was born in 1390, in Carlton on Trent, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Cromwell, was 26 and his mother, Lady Margaret, was 26. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Joane Smythe. He died on 29 March 1461, in Towton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Cromwell Lord of Carleton(이)가 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 칼턴  트렌트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Cromwell(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Joane Smythe(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1461년 3월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 타우턴에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.
